traps: amazonBedrock is a subServiceSelector parent — call addService(<providerCode>, ...) for each model provider (anthropic, amazon, meta, mistral, cohere, ai21, deepseek, lumaAi, openAi, qwen, stabilityAi, twelveLabs, writer). EstimateBuilder merges all children into a single amazonBedrock parent envelope; do NOT call addService('amazonBedrock', ...) directly — it has no fields of its own.

Each provider child has the SAME field schema (location, tierIR, model selection, token volumes, image inputs, prompt caching) but the model option ids and model-tier numeric ids differ per provider. Probe via get_service_fields(<providerCode>) before using anything other than 'anthropic'.

Anthropic field set is partitioned by 'location' (global / geo / ir) and 'tierIR' (standard / batch). Active fields use a SUFFIX matching the partition: unsuffixed = global+standard, 'IRstan' = ir+standard, 'Geostan' = geo+standard, 'IRbatch' = ir+batch, 'batch' = global+batch, 'geobatch' = geo+batch. Sending 'avgInputTokensPerRequest' (unsuffixed) when location=ir is silently ignored — use 'avgInputTokensPerRequestIRstan' instead.

Three model dropdowns must agree: 'modelSelection' (the picker, opaque sha id), 'selectedModel' (mirror, opaque sha id), and 'selectedModel_od' (numeric tier id like '107' for Sonnet 4). Sending only modelSelection without selectedModel/selectedModel_od leaves the model row blank in the rendered estimate. The id sets are NOT interchangeable — copy the matching trio from get_service_fields(anthropic).options.

selectedModel_od has known DUPLICATE ids in the manifest: 'Anthropic: Claude Sonnet 4.5' and 'Anthropic: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Long Context)' both report id='1'. Prefer Claude Sonnet 4 (selectedModel_od='107') for stable rehydration if you don't need 4.5 specifically.

Token volume fields are numericInput strings (not frequency). The calculator computes monthly tokens as avgRequestsPerMin × 60 × hoursPerDayAtThisRate × 30 × avgInputTokensPerRequest. To target 2M input tokens/month with avgRequestsPerMin='1' and hoursPerDayAtThisRate='1' (1800 req/month), set avgInputTokensPerRequest='1111'.

Prompt caching: set withPromptCaching='1' AND cacheRate='<percent>' AND cacheRead/cacheWrite to provider-model-specific opaque ids. cacheRead/cacheWrite have a SMALLER option set than modelSelection (only models that support caching) — for anthropic that excludes Sonnet 4 and earlier. To enable caching you must pick a Sonnet 4.5+ or Haiku 4.5+ model. Without all four fields surfacing together, caching does not affect cost.
imageInput / withPromptCaching are dropdown booleans encoded as STRING '1' (Yes) or '0' (No), not actual booleans. Sending true/false fails value validation.

Bedrock has a 14th sub-service-selector child: 'workload1' (a generic catch-all with templateId 'abc') that surfaces Provisioned Throughput, Model Customization, Image Generation, Embeddings, and Batch — anything that doesn't fit the per-provider on-demand inference page. Call it as addService('workload1', { ... }) — the EstimateBuilder will merge it into the same amazonBedrock parent envelope as anthropic. Adding workload1 at top-level (e.g. as its own parent) triggers the sub-service-active-list lint predicate and produces a read-only estimate.

User scenarios using the phrases 'provisioned throughput', 'caching', or 'reserved capacity' for token-volume cost optimization should map to anthropic's withPromptCaching='1' + cacheRate (a percentage discount on cached input tokens), NOT to workload1's Provisioned Throughput billing (billing_term + noOfmodels_purchased + noOfhours_used). PT is a flat hourly reservation of a model unit (~$30-40/hr for Sonnet 4) regardless of traffic; prompt caching is a per-token discount when repeated prefixes are cached. For a chatbot with 'X tokens cached', set withPromptCaching='1' and cacheRate='<X/totalInput * 100>'.

Default to location='global' for the most common 'cheapest cross-region routing' use case. The unsuffixed field family (modelSelection, avgInputTokensPerRequest, etc.) only applies under location='global'+tierIR='standard'. Choosing location='ir' (In Region — slightly higher per-token price for guaranteed in-region inference) requires using the IRstan-suffixed family (modelSelectionIRstan, avgInputTokensPerRequestIRstan, etc.) — DIFFERENT field IDs, not just different values.

required.hint: Inference route. Determines which suffixed family of token-volume fields is active.
required.shape: string id from { global | geo | ir }

required.hint: On-demand vs batch. Standard = pay-per-token; batch = bulk records.
Model picker (opaque sha id). Must match the same Claude SKU as selectedModel and selectedModel_od.
Mirror of modelSelection (opaque sha id). MUST be set or the model row stays blank.
Numeric tier id for the on-demand price book (e.g. '107' for Sonnet 4).
Average inference requests per minute (unsuffixed = global+standard).
Hours per day at the avgRequestsPerMin rate.
Average input tokens per request. Combine with rate × hours to back into monthly token volume.
Model picker (opaque sha id). workload1 has its own modelSelection options covering ALL providers; pick one matching the model you reserved.
Mirror of modelSelection. workload1's selectedModel option ids differ from anthropic's even for the same Claude SKU — re-probe.
Number of model units (MUs) reserved per month.
Hours per month each MU is billed (720 = always-on for 1-month commit).
